answer:
\boxed{A}

The lateral cutaneous nerve of the thigh (Lateral Femoral Cutaneous Nerve) innervates the anterolateral thigh skin, and its injury would explain the postoperative pain and soreness in that region. The tacker was placed below and lateral to the **ilio-pubic tract**, which aligns with the course of this nerve. The other options (ilioinguinal, genitofemoral, and obturator nerves) innervate different regions (groin, genitalia, or medial thigh) and are less likely to cause thigh pain in this scenario.
